vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1257: Health classifier: classifyAgentKind misclassifies review/test/ship specialists as 'work', breaking workAgentCount cap

plan.title: Health classifier: classifyAgentKind misclassifies review/test/ship specialists as 'work', breaking workAgentCount cap
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: PAN-1257 was closed in PAN-1249 with the production fix to classifyAgentKind (taking an optional `role` argument so agent-prefixed specialists are no longer counted as 'work' agents). The issue was reopened on 2026-05-24 because the original acceptance criteria required unit tests covering review/ship/work/slot role values, proof that health.summary.workAgentCount excludes specialists, and a swarm-capacity regression test — none of which exist. Without that test coverage, a future refactor can silently regress the classifier and re-introduce the dispatch-blocking bug.
narratives.Proposal: Test-only work: (1) export classifyAgentKind and add a focused unit test covering every Role value plus legacy prefixes and slot agent IDs; (2) add a summary-level integration test that drives getSystemHealthSnapshot with a mixed mock agent list and asserts workAgentCount, specialistSessionCount, and planningAgentCount; (3) extend src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/swarm.test.ts with a regression case proving dispatch is not blocked when only specialists are running for other issues.

autoDecisions.summary: Export classifyAgentKind directly from system-health-service.ts (matching the file's existing `export function readGlobalResourceConfig` / `export function getResourceConfig` style) rather than introducing a __testInternals barrel.
autoDecisions.rationale: Single helper, no need for a barrel; the existing file already exports several utility functions the same way. JSDoc note flags it as test-internal.
Matches the existing __tests__ layout next to other service tests (agent-output-service.test.ts, config.test.ts).

autoDecisions.summary: Use a parameterized it.each table for the classifier role matrix.
autoDecisions.rationale: Maps cleanly to the issue's enumerated acceptance criteria and stays readable as cases are added (review subroles, slot IDs, legacy prefixes).

autoDecisions.summary: For the summary-level test, mock the I/O dependencies (listRunningAgents, getAgentRuntimeState, listPaneValues, readProcessTable, DockerStatsCollector) rather than spin up the full environment.
autoDecisions.rationale: collectAgentProcesses cannot be exercised against the real OS in a unit test; following the mock-the-edges pattern from agent-output-service.test.ts is the project standard.

autoDecisions.summary: Extend the existing src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/swarm.test.ts with the new regression case rather than create a new file.
autoDecisions.rationale: All required mocks (getSystemHealthSnapshot, getResourceConfig, vbrief, agents, tmux, projects, tracker-utils, child_process) are already wired up in that file; a separate file would duplicate ~200 lines of scaffolding.

autoDecisions.summary: Do not modify production code in this issue. classifyAgentKind already implements the correct behavior and the caller already passes agent.role.
autoDecisions.rationale: The reopen comment is explicit that 'the implementation now passes agent.role into the classifier' — only tests are missing.

items.title: Export classifyAgentKind and add direct unit tests for the role matrix

narrative.Action:
In src/dashboard/server/services/system-health-service.ts, change `function classifyAgentKind` to `export function classifyAgentKind` and add a JSDoc comment marking it `@internal — exported for unit tests`. Create src/dashboard/server/services/__tests__/system-health-classifier.test.ts with a parameterized table covering every role value the function must handle.

Required test cases (use it.each):
- ('agent-pan-1228-review', 'review') → 'specialist'
- ('agent-pan-1228-review-correctness', 'review') → 'specialist'
- ('agent-pan-1228-review-performance', 'review') → 'specialist'
- ('agent-pan-1228-review-requirements', 'review') → 'specialist'
- ('agent-pan-1228-review-security', 'review') → 'specialist'
- ('agent-pan-1228-ship', 'ship') → 'specialist'
- ('agent-pan-1190-test', 'test') → 'specialist'
- ('agent-pan-1190', 'work') → 'work'
- ('agent-pan-1122-1', 'work') → 'work'  // slot agent
- ('agent-pan-1228', undefined) → 'work'  // legacy state file with no role field
- ('planning-abc123', undefined) → 'planning'
- ('specialist-foo', undefined) → 'specialist'
- ('foo-agent', undefined) → 'specialist'  // *-agent fallback
- ('random-thing', undefined) → 'other'

For each case, assert the return value with `expect(classifyAgentKind(id, role)).toBe(expectedKind)`. No mocking required — this is a pure function.

subItems.title: classifyAgentKind is exported from src/dashboard/server/services/system-health-service.ts with a JSDoc `@internal` note
system-health-classifier.test.ts exists at src/dashboard/server/services/__tests__/ and runs under the existing vitest configuration
Test covers role values: 'work', 'review', 'review-correctness', 'review-performance', 'review-requirements', 'review-security', 'test', 'ship', and undefined
`agent-pan-1228-review` with role='review' classifies as 'specialist'
`agent-pan-1228-ship` with role='ship' classifies as 'specialist'
`agent-pan-1190` with role='work' classifies as 'work'
`agent-pan-1122-1` (slot agent) with role='work' classifies as 'work'
`agent-pan-1228` with role=undefined (legacy state file) classifies as 'work'
Legacy prefixes `planning-*` → 'planning', `specialist-*` → 'specialist', `*-agent` suffix → 'specialist', unrecognized IDs → 'other'
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test pass for the new file

items.title: Add summary-level test proving workAgentCount excludes specialists in getSystemHealthSnapshot

narrative.Action:
Add src/dashboard/server/services/__tests__/system-health-summary-counts.test.ts that drives getSystemHealthSnapshot end-to-end with mocked I/O and asserts the snapshot's summary counts.

Mock surface (follow the pattern in src/dashboard/server/services/__tests__/agent-output-service.test.ts):
- vi.mock('../../../../lib/agents.js', ...) with listRunningAgents returning Effect.succeed(mixedAgents)
- Mock getAgentRuntimeState to return Effect.succeed(null) for all agents
- Mock listPaneValues to return Effect.succeed(['0']) so panePid resolves to 0 and descendant lookup short-circuits
- Mock readProcessTable (the helper used by collectAgentProcesses) to return an empty Map
- Mock DockerStatsCollector or stub the docker stats path to return zero containers

Mixed agent fixture (each with `status: 'running'` so the filter at line 495 keeps them):
- { id: 'agent-pan-1190', role: 'work', issueId: 'PAN-1190', tmuxActive: true, ... }
- { id: 'agent-pan-1122-1', role: 'work', issueId: 'PAN-1122', tmuxActive: true, ... }  // slot
- { id: 'agent-pan-1228-review', role: 'review', issueId: 'PAN-1228', tmuxActive: true, ... }
- { id: 'agent-pan-1228-review-correctness', role: 'review', issueId: 'PAN-1228', tmuxActive: true, ... }
- { id: 'agent-pan-1228-ship', role: 'ship', issueId: 'PAN-1228', tmuxActive: true, ... }
- { id: 'agent-pan-1190-test', role: 'test', issueId: 'PAN-1190', tmuxActive: true, ... }
- { id: 'planning-abc', role: 'plan' as any, issueId: 'PAN-9999', tmuxActive: true, ... }  // legacy prefix

Assertions:
- snapshot.summary.workAgentCount === 2 (the two work agents only)
- snapshot.summary.specialistSessionCount === 4 (two reviews, one ship, one test)
- snapshot.summary.planningAgentCount === 1 (the legacy planning-* agent)
- snapshot.summary.agentCount === 7

Reset module state between tests with vi.resetModules() + dynamic import, or call any test-only reset helper if one is added. If module-level memoization (cachedHealth, previousSeverity) interferes, document and address it (see Hazard H2).

subItems.title: New test file exists at src/dashboard/server/services/__tests__/system-health-summary-counts.test.ts
Test drives getSystemHealthSnapshot() (not just the private classifier) so it exercises the full collectAgentProcesses → summary derivation path

With a mocked listRunningAgents returning a mix of role='work' (including a slot agent), role='review', role='ship', role='test', plus a legacy `planning-*` ID, the snapshot's summary.workAgentCount equals exactly the number of role='work' agents

Under the same fixture, summary.specialistSessionCount equals the number of review/ship/test agents (i.e. > 0 — confirming the inverse of the original PAN-1257 bug where specialistSessionCount was always 0)
summary.planningAgentCount counts only legacy `planning-*`-prefixed agents
Module-level memoization (cachedHealth, previousSeverity, candidateSeverity) does not cause cross-test bleed — test passes both alone and in the full suite
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test pass for the new file

items.title: Add swarm dispatch regression: dispatch succeeds when specialists run for other issues

narrative.Action:
Extend src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/swarm.test.ts with a new `it(...)` case proving that a swarm dispatch is NOT rejected with 429 'No agent capacity available' when only specialists are running for other issues.

The existing test file already mocks getSystemHealthSnapshot and getResourceConfig (see lines 24-27 and 196-201). Add a new case that:
- Sets getResourceConfig to return { agentBlockCount: 4 } (the existing default in this file)
- Sets getSystemHealthSnapshot to return summary.workAgentCount = 1 (i.e. 1 active work agent, leaving 3 slots free — modelling the PAN-1257 repro state where 7 specialists were running and the original bug reported 8/10 work agents)
- Calls the swarm dispatch handler and asserts the response status is NOT 429 and the body does not include 'No agent capacity available'

The assertion deliberately does NOT depend on swarm fully succeeding — only that the workAgentCount-based capacity check at swarm.ts:988-999 lets the request through. If the call would have to traverse much more swarm code than is mocked, prefer an assertion on the dispatch outcome that proves the early-return at the capacity gate did not fire (for instance, that mocks downstream of the capacity gate were called, or that the response is one of the later success/validation responses).

Document the test with a comment referencing PAN-1257 and the original repro line: 'Failed: No agent capacity available (10/10 agents running)' with 7 of those being specialists.

subItems.title: New `it(...)` case exists in src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/swarm.test.ts referencing PAN-1257 in the test name or a comment
Test mocks getSystemHealthSnapshot to return a summary with workAgentCount strictly less than agentBlockCount (e.g. workAgentCount=1, agentBlockCount=4)
Test asserts the swarm dispatch handler does NOT return HTTP 429 with 'No agent capacity available'
Test passes consistently (no flakes) under npm test
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test pass for the modified file
